blocks.body: Two-object verbs (verbs with two objects) — give, send, offer, tell, show — have an indirect object (to whom) and a direct object (what). In English, both can become the subject of a passive sentence: "He was given a book" or "A book was given to him".

{ "title": "7. Short summary", "items": [ "Two-object verbs have IO (to whom) and DO (what): give, send, offer, tell, show.", "Two variants of the passive: a person as a subject (more often) or a thing as a subject.", "He was given a book. is a more natural option (the person is the subject).", "A book was given to him. — when the emphasis is on things.", "Preposition to — for transfer, for — \"for the benefit of.\"", "Do not add a preposition when the person is the subject of the passive." ] }
